codeReview.summary: The celebration feature is well-implemented and meets all stated requirements. The Celebration component correctly renders confetti particles via CSS animations with a glowing BINGO banner, respects prefers-reduced-motion (both via JS hook and CSS fallback), uses pointer-events-none to avoid blocking interaction, and is driven purely by the reactive hasBingo prop ensuring correct start/stop/re-trigger behavior. One non-blocking visual quality issue was identified: confetti particles regenerate on every re-render (e.g., when toggling a square during celebration), causing a visual flicker.

issues.description: generateParticles() is called directly in the render body of ConfettiOverlay, not memoized. Every time the parent re-renders (e.g., user toggles a square during celebration), all 40 confetti particles regenerate with new random positions/sizes/colors, causing all particles to visually 'jump' to new positions. This should be wrapped in useState or useMemo to preserve particle identity across re-renders while bingo holds. Fix: `const [particles] = useState(() => generateParticles(PARTICLE_COUNT))` inside ConfettiOverlay.

The test 'banner has visually distinct styling from normal state' only asserts `expect(alert.textContent).toBeTruthy()`, which doesn't meaningfully verify visual distinctness. Every other test already verifies the alert has text content. A more meaningful assertion would check for the 'celebration-glow' CSS class or the specific styling classes that differentiate the banner from normal state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md 'Key Files' section lists paths with `app/` prefix (e.g., `app/routes/__root.tsx`, `app/lib/bingo.ts`, `app/components/Celebration.tsx`) but the actual project uses `src/` prefix (e.g., `src/routes/`, `src/lib/`, `src/components/`). This could confuse future workers who reference AGENTS.md for file locations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md lists `app/routes/__root.tsx`, `app/routes/index.tsx`, `app/lib/bingo.ts`, `app/components/Board.tsx`, `app/components/Celebration.tsx`. Actual files are at `src/routes/__root.tsx`, `src/routes/index.tsx`, `src/lib/bingo.ts`, `src/components/Board.tsx`, `src/components/Celebration.tsx`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker created vitest.config.ts and vitest.setup.ts as testing infrastructure in this commit, and used the `@vitest-environment jsdom` docblock pattern in the test file. Neither the testing setup nor the jsdom environment pattern is documented in AGENTS.md or the library. Future workers writing React component tests need to know about this pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Celebration.test.tsx line 2: `// @vitest-environment jsdom`. vitest.config.ts sets `environment: 'node'` as default (overridden per-file). vitest.setup.ts imports `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest` for custom matchers. AGENTS.md mentions 'bun test' but not the vitest config setup or jsdom pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `@testing-library/jest-dom` package was added as a dev dependency in this commit, providing matchers like `toBeInTheDocument()` and `toHaveTextContent()`. The environment.md library file does not document this testing utility. Future workers should know it's available for component testing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json added `@testing-library/jest-dom: ^6.9.1` in this commit. vitest.setup.ts imports `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest`. These matchers are used in Celebration.test.tsx but not documented in .factory/library/environment.md.

summary: The celebration feature passes review. All seven expected behaviors are correctly implemented: bingo triggers confetti + glowing banner, celebration persists while bingo holds (no timeout), stops when broken, doesn't block interaction (pointer-events-none overlay), re-triggers on re-achievement, is visually distinct, and respects prefers-reduced-motion with both a JS hook and CSS media query fallback. The component is well-structured with clean separation (Celebration, ConfettiOverlay, useReducedMotion hook). 9 tests cover the core behaviors including reduced-motion support and accessibility. Two non-blocking issues were noted: confetti particles regenerate on every re-render causing a visual flicker (should be memoized), and one test assertion is too weak to meaningfully verify visual distinctness.
